cards.title: Apple Vision Pro VP Joins OpenAI Hardware Team
cards.body: Paul Meade, Apple's VP overseeing Vision Pro, is reportedly leaving to join OpenAI's hardware division. The move signals OpenAI's intensifying focus on building custom hardware.

cards.title: Asian AI Startups Launch Mythos-Competitive Models
cards.body: New Asian AI models promise Mythos-like capabilities without export ban risks. The development could shift a massive market away from U.S. AI labs.

cards.title: Trump Admin Authorizes Mythos 5 for 100+ Organizations
cards.body: Over 100 U.S. companies and government agencies are now authorized to use Anthropic's Mythos 5, including their non-American employees. The clearance represents a significant expansion of model access.

cards.title: OpenAI Limits GPT-5.6 Rollout Over Safety Concerns
cards.body: OpenAI is restricting GPT-5.6 to select partners following government pressure. The company argues such restrictions shouldn't become standard practice.

cards.title: OpenAI Announces Jalapeño Custom Inference Chip
cards.body: OpenAI unveiled Jalapeño, a custom inference chip developed with Broadcom, challenging Nvidia's AI chip dominance. The move joins Google, Apple, and SpaceX in building proprietary AI silicon.

cards.title: OpenAI Hires Uber India Chief for Market Leadership
cards.body: OpenAI recruited Uber India's chief to lead operations in its biggest non-U.S. market. The hire accelerates OpenAI's expansion through new offices, partnerships, and hiring.

cards.title: Patronus AI Raises $50M for AI Agent Testing
cards.body: Agent-testing startup Patronus AI secured $50M, founded by former Meta AI researchers. The company is seeing unprecedented demand for stress-testing digital AI agents.

cards.title: Claude Wins Paid Consumer Market Share From ChatGPT
cards.body: Anthropic's Claude is increasingly attracting paid subscribers despite ChatGPT's market dominance. Data shows consumers choosing Claude as their preferred paid AI assistant.

cards.title: General Intuition Raises $320M for Gaming-Trained AI
cards.body: General Intuition secured $320M to scale AI trained on millions of gameplay hours. The company believes action data from games can develop more human-like AI intuition.

cards.title: Databricks' Un-0 Could Cut AI Power Consumption 1000x
cards.body: Un-0, an image-generation tool from Databricks' former AI chief, demonstrates 1000x potential power efficiency improvements. The technology replicates conventional AI systems with dramatically reduced computational costs.

cards.title: Netris Raises $15M Series A from a16z
cards.body: Netris secured $15M to accelerate neocloud deployment with network switch software. The platform helps operators reduce time-to-live significantly.

cards.title: Adobe Acquires Video Enhancement Tool Topaz Labs
cards.body: Adobe is acquiring Topaz Labs to integrate its image and video enhancement capabilities across Adobe apps. The acquisition strengthens Adobe's AI-powered creative tools suite.

cards.title: Amazon Invests Additional $13B in India AI Infrastructure
cards.body: Amazon committed $13B for fresh India AI infrastructure investments. The move joins global tech companies racing to expand AI capabilities in India.

cards.title: Europe Challenges Washington's Semiconductor Export Restrictions
cards.body: Europe is pushing back against U.S. chip restrictions through the MATCH Act. ASML CEO Christophe Fouquet highlighted tensions over limiting older chipmaking equipment exports.

cards.title: AI Model Capabilities Now Have Political Consequences
cards.body: AI advancement has progressed beyond corporate competition into politically consequential territory. Addressing these impacts requires collective action beyond individual companies.

cards.title: Apple Vision Pro Exec Joins OpenAI Hardware Team
cards.body: Paul Meade, Apple's VP overseeing Vision Pro, is reportedly leaving to join OpenAI's hardware division. The move signals OpenAI's expanding ambitions beyond software into physical devices.

cards.title: Cancer Patient Uses Claude AI to Fight Disease
cards.body: Connor Christou fed his medical data—blood results, scans, wearables, and journals—into Claude to help combat his cancer diagnosis. The approach demonstrates AI's potential in personalized healthcare applications.

cards.title: Asian AI Startups Launch Models Amid Export Bans
cards.body: New Asian AI models promise capabilities similar to Anthropic's Mythos without export ban risks. U.S. AI labs may lose significant market share in the region.

cards.title: Trump Admin Authorizes Mythos Use by 100+ Entities
cards.body: Over 100 U.S. companies and government agencies are now authorized to use Mythos 5, including non-American employees. The authorization represents a significant expansion of enterprise AI access.

cards.title: OpenAI Resists Government Restrictions on GPT-5.6
cards.body: OpenAI limited GPT-5.6's rollout after a government request, but opposes making such access processes the norm. The company argues restrictions prevent critical tools from reaching developers and defenders.

cards.title: OpenAI Hires Uber India Chief for Market Expansion
cards.body: OpenAI appointed a new leader from Uber India to spearhead its growth in the region. The hire marks OpenAI's aggressive push into India with expanded offices and partnerships.

cards.title: OpenAI Launches Jalapeño Chip to Challenge Nvidia
cards.body: OpenAI unveiled Jalapeño, a custom inference chip built with Broadcom, ending its total dependence on Nvidia. Major tech companies including Google, Apple, and SpaceX are similarly building proprietary chips.

cards.title: AI Models Now Have Real Political Consequences
cards.body: AI capabilities have advanced to the point where they carry significant political implications. Addressing these consequences requires collective action beyond individual company competition.

cards.title: White House Asks OpenAI to Slow GPT-5.6 Release
cards.body: The Trump administration requested OpenAI limit GPT-5.6's rollout to select partners due to safety concerns. OpenAI complied, restricting the broader public release of its newest model.

cards.title: Patronus AI Raises $50M for AI Agent Testing
cards.body: Agent-testing startup Patronus AI, founded by ex-Meta researchers, secured $50M to build digital stress-test environments. The company is experiencing unprecedented demand from enterprises.

cards.title: Claude Gains Ground Over ChatGPT Among Paid Users
cards.body: Despite ChatGPT's market dominance, Anthropic's Claude is increasingly winning over paying consumers. The shift shows growing consumer preference for Claude's capabilities and design.

cards.title: Video Game Training Could Revolutionize AI Agents
cards.body: General Intuition raised $320M to train AI on millions of hours of gameplay footage. The company believes action data from games can help AI develop human-like intuition.

cards.title: Databricks Aims to Cut AI Power Consumption 1000x
cards.body: Databricks' former AI chief developed Un-0, an image-generation tool that drastically reduces computational overhead. The technology demonstrates how to replicate AI systems with minimal power.

cards.title: Netris Raises $15M to Accelerate Neocloud Launches
cards.body: Netris secured Series A funding from a16z for network infrastructure software enabling faster AI neocloud deployments. The platform helps operators reduce time-to-live significantly.

cards.title: Adobe Acquires Topaz Labs for AI Enhancement Tools
cards.body: Adobe announced its acquisition of Topaz Labs, maker of AI-powered image and video enhancement tools. The integration will expand Adobe's AI capabilities across its creative suite.

cards.title: Amazon Invests $13B More in India AI Infrastructure
cards.body: Amazon announced a fresh $13B investment in India for AI infrastructure expansion. The move joins global tech giants racing to build AI capacity in the region.

cards.title: OpenAI Restrictions Mark Unprecedented Government Intervention
cards.body: The Trump administration's request to limit GPT-5.6 distribution represents an unprecedented level of government control over AI deployment. OpenAI expressed concerns this could become standard practice.

cards.title: AI Reshaping Retail Behind-the-Scenes Operations
cards.body: AI is transforming how retailers make decisions on product placement, search results, and inventory. The changes are less visible than chatbots but fundamentally alter how retail works.

cards.title: Stripe and OpenAI Back Respiratory Infection Research
cards.body: Stripe and Anthropic partnered with OpenAI to fund new research combating respiratory infections. The collaboration applies AI and computational resources to develop preventive treatments.

cards.title: Web Data Infrastructure Layer Emerging for AI
cards.body: Enterprises need structured data at scale to maximize AI potential, creating demand for a new web data infrastructure layer. Companies are building tools to unlock blocked and unstructured data.

cards.title: NYT Accuses Microsoft of Copyright Infringement for OpenAI
cards.body: The New York Times criticized Microsoft for building a copyright-infringing supercomputer supporting OpenAI's operations. The lawsuit alleges unauthorized use of copyrighted content in AI training.

cards.title: Notion Shutters Email App as AI Agents Replace It
cards.body: Notion discontinued its Skiff-influenced email app because most users switched to AI agents instead. The shift reflects growing preference for AI-powered email handling over traditional clients.

cards.title: Anthropic Demands Punishment for Alibaba Claude Cloning
cards.body: Anthropic accused Alibaba of executing the largest Claude model cloning attack to date. The company called for formal penalties against the unauthorized AI model replication.
